title: Best Python Libraries for PDF Table Extraction: Developer's Technical Guide

h1: Best Python Libraries for PDF Table Extraction: Technical Comparison

hero.headline: Compare Python Libraries for PDF Table Extraction
hero.subheadline: Technical analysis of tabula-py, camelot, pdfplumber, and PyPDF2 with code examples, performance data, and real-world limitations

summary: This guide compares the most popular Python libraries for extracting tables from PDF documents, including tabula-py, camelot-py, pdfplumber, and PyPDF2. Each library has distinct strengths: tabula-py excels with complex layouts, camelot offers precise table detection, pdfplumber handles text-based tables well, and PyPDF2 works for simple structured documents. We cover installation requirements, code examples, performance characteristics, and specific limitations to help developers choose the right tool for their PDF processing needs.

faq.question: Which Python library works best for scanned PDF table extraction?
faq.answer: Camelot-py with lattice mode works well for scanned PDFs with visible table borders, but you'll need to preprocess with OCR tools like pytesseract for text recognition. tabula-py also handles scanned documents but requires Java dependencies.

faq.question: Can these libraries handle tables that span multiple PDF pages?
faq.answer: Most libraries struggle with multi-page tables. tabula-py offers page range parameters, and pdfplumber allows manual page iteration, but you'll often need custom logic to merge results and handle split headers correctly.

faq.question: What's the performance difference between tabula-py and camelot for large PDFs?
faq.answer: tabula-py generally processes faster due to its Java backend, especially on multi-page documents. Camelot's lattice detection is more CPU-intensive but often more accurate for complex table structures.

faq.question: Do these libraries work with password-protected or encrypted PDFs?
faq.answer: PyPDF2 and pdfplumber can handle password-protected PDFs if you provide the password programmatically. tabula-py and camelot may require decrypting the PDF first using other tools before processing.
